hero.title:
Why
Vichada?
hero.subtitle: Among our explorations to concentrate Esperantists in specific places, Vichada appears as an interesting possibility due to its unique geographic and legal conditions. However, this project is just one of many initiatives in our global network.

territory.title:
Reasons to
explore Vichada
territory.subtitle: We are studying various places worldwide. Vichada is one of the options being considered due to specific advantages.

01.title: Size + demographic emptiness
01.subtitle: Size + demographic emptiness
01.body: ~100,000 km² with ~80,000 inhabitants. Very low density — possibility to organize without social pressure.

02.title: Flat savannah
02.subtitle: Flat savannah — accessible development
02.body: Almost flat topography — roads, water systems, energy, and urbanism more accessible than in mountainous terrain.

03.title: Strategic water
03.subtitle: Strategic water — Orinoco basin
03.body: Rivers, streams, aquifers, and stable rainfall — important resource for future development.

04.title: Legal framework: ZIDRES
04.subtitle: Legal framework for development
04.body: There is a legal framework for organized development projects in the region.

05.title: Distance from urban centers
05.subtitle: Distance from urban centers
05.body: Less urban pressure. Possibility to create an organized community with its own identity.

06.title: Border with Venezuela
06.subtitle: Border with Venezuela
06.body: Geopolitical position with potential for transnational cooperation.

07.title: Available land
07.subtitle: Available land
07.body: Possibility to acquire land legally for long-term development.

08.title: Solar energy potential
08.subtitle: Solar energy potential
08.body: High radiation and perfect terrain for renewable energy sources.

09.title: Fresh start
09.subtitle: Fresh start opportunity
09.body: Possibility to plan and organize without previous limitations from existing infrastructure.

conclusion.title: Our Approach
conclusion.description: Vichada is one of several places we are studying to concentrate Esperantists. Our focus is to help the global network of 2 million speakers in 180 countries — through support networks, living spaces, and organizational tools.

scaleTests.title: What we want to test
scaleTests.subtitle: Exploration goals
scaleTests.description: Places like Vichada allow us to explore:

growth.title:
Long-term
perspective.
growth.subtitle: Future
growth.description: Our project is not just about one place. It's about creating a network of places where Esperantists can support each other for decades.

colombia.subtitle: Strategic considerations
colombia.description: And Vichada offers a unique combination of factors.

home.subtitle: We are organizing the first network-nation for Esperantists — 2 million people in 180 countries. Create your support networks, find living spaces, collaborate on projects, and join a global movement that transcends borders.

manifesto.title: ESPERANTISTUJO MANIFESTO
manifesto.subtitle: From Dispersion to Global Network
manifesto.tagline: 2 Million Citizens • 180 Countries • One Common Language

sections.title: THE OPPORTUNITY: 2 MILLION SPEAKERS WITHOUT ORGANIZATION
sections.content:
Today there are approximately 2 million Esperantists in 180 countries. However, they remain scattered — without a common platform to organize themselves, help each other, or collaborate on concrete projects.

The current situation shows:

1. **Lack of organization** — talents and resources are not coordinated
2. **Isolation** — Esperantists don't know about other Esperantists in their city or country
3. **No common infrastructure** — there is no tool to easily find housing, work, or collaboration
4. **Weak voice** — 2 million people with almost no political or economic influence

We propose transforming this dispersion into an **organized network**.

sections.title: OUR VISION: NETWORK-NATION OF ESPERANTISTS
sections.content:
We imagine a global network where Esperantists can:

• **Find each other** in their city, country, or worldwide
• **Create support networks** — housing, work, travel, emergencies
• **Organize locally** — neighborhoods, co-housing, cooperatives
• **Collaborate on projects** — from local initiatives to global campaigns
• **Become citizens** of a network nation with its own tools and systems

This is not a political party nor a formal state. It's a **practical network** to organize ourselves and function together.

We are also exploring the possibility of concentrating Esperantists in specific places (e.g. Vichada, Colombia) — but that is just **one of many initiatives** in our broader strategy.

sections.title: HOW IT WORKS
sections.content:
1. **Citizenship Platform**
   Open-source system where citizens can register, find each other, and organize themselves.

2. **Support Networks**
   Help Esperantists find housing, work, travel partners, or emergency help.

3. **Local Organization**
   Create neighborhoods, co-housing, cooperatives in your cities.

4. **Projects and Funding**
   Propose, fund, and realize projects with full transparency — from events to infrastructure.

5. **Citizenship Fee**
   Initially through donations, later through voluntary fees to support the network and its services.

sections.title: HOW TO JOIN
sections.content:
**First phase:** Register as a citizen and complete your profile.

**Second phase:** Connect with Esperantists in your region or interests.

**Third phase:** Collaborate on projects, support others, or start your own.

This is practical organization:
what happens when 2 million people start working together?

sections.title: CALL TO ACTION
sections.content:
We are not creating a new country.
We are organizing an existing community.

1. Join as a **citizen** and complete your profile.
2. Find other Esperantists in your city.
3. Start or join projects.
4. Help build the network.

Humanity already has 2 million Esperantists.
We just need to organize ourselves.

position.title: Our position
position.subtitle: Practical organization, not utopian dream.
position.zamenhofText1: Zamenhof didn't just create a language — he imagined
position.zamenhofText2: humanism: the idea that people can transcend national borders and organize themselves according to shared values.
position.zamenhofText3: He knew that language alone was not enough. The vision was that people would actually
position.zamenhofText5: — not just in congresses, but in everyday life.
position.zamenhofText6: Esperantistujo is an attempt to realize that vision today
position.zamenhofText7: — using modern tools to organize 2 million speakers in a global network of collaboration and support.
